mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS stays near the zero lower bound and CPIAUCSL is still rising, equities benefit from negative real policy rates and lower discount rates, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show positive 20d SPX returns above the unconditional baseline, or if the signal loses directional accuracy after the Fed begins a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE from already elevated levels signals weakening household income and demand, which depresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if, after UNRATE crosses the trigger, SPX posts positive 20d returns in most cases or the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue growth, which lifts near-term earnings revisions and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly changes in RSAFS do not correspond to above-baseline 20d SPX returns over rolling multi-year samples, or if the sign reverses during normal expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained improvement in INDPRO signals stronger industrial output and operating leverage, which raises earnings optimism and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month production uptrends fail to produce positive 60d SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears once recessionary periods are excluded.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in HOUST signals a housing-led recovery in credit-sensitive demand, and that broadens growth expectations enough to lift S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if strong year-over-year housing starts growth does not coincide with positive 20d SPX performance, or if the signal is inverted in non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises, payroll growth is deteriorating and firms are likely to cut earnings guidance, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-down and UNRATE-up condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered cases fail to produce below-baseline 20d SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS rate suppresses risk appetite and raises discount rates, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days once policy moves above neutral.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 2% do not produce materially lower 60d SPX returns than lower-rate periods, or if the sign is not consistently negative during tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling RSAFS and rising UNRATE captures a demand shock that hits both sales and labor income, and this joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint sales-and-labor deterioration does not improve predictive power versus single-series rules, or if 20d SPX returns remain positive in a majority of triggered episodes.
